title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
abstract: We study how vision-language models trained on internet-scale data can be incorporated directly into end-to-end robot control to boost generalization and enable emergent semantic reasoning. Our method co-fine-tunes state-of-the-art vision-language models on both robotic trajectory data and internet-scale vision-language tasks, such that it can map robot observations to actions. This generalizes the application of such models to robot control and improves over non-web-pretrained models. RT-2 shows improved generalization to novel objects, backgrounds, and environments, as well as emergent capabilities such as reasoning and symbol understanding, even when trained on relatively small amounts of in-domain robot data.
introduction_excerpt: Large models pretrained on internet-scale data have demonstrated remarkable capabilities, including following complex instructions, engaging in multi-step reasoning, generating coherent long-form text, and understanding novel concepts in vision and language. Can such capabilities be transferred to robotic systems to enable open-vocabulary robot control? We present RT-2, a novel approach that uses vision-language models as the backbone for a robotics policy, where actions are represented as language tokens and generated by the same model that processes vision and language.
